prompt:
Hey! I completely understand you and feel sorry you have to experience the lackage of free time. It's an awfull state, when your life seems not to belong to yourself. I faced  with the same problem during my first year in the university and here are some tips that helped me to organise my free time and to lead a harmonical study-work-life balance:

First of all, timetables really work. If you have a variety of things you should do, try to make an emphasis on the most significant ones and prioritise all task accoarding to the dependance on their importance and urgency. Then you should make a scedule for yourself, concidering all the details of your own limitations in time, mood, desire to study and moral state. For example, some people are more productive in the morning, whereas the others - in the evening, or even at night. Also this timetable will help you to remember all your deadlines, to be more prepared for all control works and to do everything in time.

Secondly, don't forget about your social life and communication! Choose a couple of hours in a week, in which you allow yourself just to have a rest. You can meet your friends, read a book, do some hobby - whatever you want, but it shouldn't be conncted with your university at all. During these hours you won't lose a lot of time, instead you get motivation, happiness and relax.

And the last advice - always remember, that your assesments don't determine your personality. In a couple of years you will hardly remember a bad mark for a control work, about which you worry now. However, time flows very fast and your university life will come to the end quite soon. Try to live this chapter of your life in a such way, that in the future it will evoke only happy and exciting memories!

TASK: In your Telegram group students are discussing this question:  How to study well at MIPT? Write your answer to this question. Give 3-4  recommendations.
STUDENT_TEXT: I think that for study well at MIPT is important to study a lot. You must offen lerning fisics and match. I recomend for everybody practis sport and read books. I know that the study at MIPT is very hard, but is important to finish this university. I can say, that for good studing you can ask help to his friends. For conclusion i want to recomend sleep and eat good.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

TASK: There is an opinion that too many young people now want to get a university degree. It would be better for society if most of them start working after finishing secondary school. How much do you agree with this point of view? State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.
STUDENT_TEXT: Nowadays alomost every person in developed world can read and write. It is the base level of knowloge for us. And this is good, but also many of us have (or will have) university degree. University degree become base level. Does it good?

I think that procces not so good, as seemed on the surface, because of the several reason.

Firstly, there is not real free (even in Russia or in another "social country"). There is always budget  formed by government with collected taxes. And university education is not cheap. Do you agree that someone that you dont now get such a great gift with your money?

Secondary, when somewhat become free, it
TEXT_TYPE: essay

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. I hope my tips will be useful for you.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hi! I very understand your feelings. The problem you faced with is very familiar to me. At the begining of the first year in the university it was hard to me to cope with numerous of deadlines, tests, lectures. So I'll try to give you some tips.

First of all make a note with all things you need to do. When you add new thing, write a deadline, name of this thing and approximate time you need to do it. Then you should study planning your week. I recommend you to look at your note on the Sundays and make a plan for the following week. The hardest thing is to follow this plan, but I hope you'll make it. 

I hope my tips'll help you to improve your time-management skills and you'll have more free time.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

TASK: Some people think that parents should teach their children how to be good members of society. Others, however, believe that school is the best place to learn this. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
STUDENT_TEXT: Some would say that parents should teach their offspring how to be good members of society, while others are of the opinion that school is the best in this regard. This essay agrees with the latter point and will show that, despite the practical experiences that parents give their children, school lessons can give deep insights into what it takes to be good citizens. Some believe that parents can educate their children about being good members of society based on their life experiences. This is because the life experiences that parents can give their children are straightforward, down-to-earth, and so they can easily apply what their parents teach them in reality. For example, many children in Thailand become more polite, honest, and caring to everyone as a direct result of the practical lessons that their parents give them at home. However, I believe that parents now are so busy and do not spend much time with their children teaching them. Lessons at school can provide children with valuable insights into being good members of society. In class, students can receive lessons about different traits of a truly good person that society needs, and then they put what they learn into practice by creating real-life problems and solving them together. For instance, after receiving lessons in civic education at school, many Vietnamese students are more willing to help their neighbors and even strangers, and they feel extremely happy after doing something good for others. For this reason, I believe that school lessons are more influential to young children. In conclusion, despite the practical experiences that parents can give their children at home, this essay believes that school lessons can help students deepen their understanding of being good members of society.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

TASK: Some people think that parents should teach their children how to be good members of society. Others, however, believe that school is the best place to learn this. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
STUDENT_TEXT: Some people believe that children should be taught by their parents about how to function as useful members of society, while others believe that sending children to educational institutions is the best way for them to study this. Although the latter opinion can be beneficial in some cases, I believe that family upbringing plays a more important role in educating children to be good parts of the community. Schools can be considered suitable places for children to learn to be good citizens. With standardized educational methods, schools can foster children’s cognitive development so that they are able to contribute to society in the future. For example, in Russia there are a number of state and private schools that a famous for the high quality of education they give to students. However, not all aspiring families manage to place their children in these schools. The lucky ones who graduate from them often become successful and well-known members of society contributing to different spheres of economics, science, humanities and arts. Still, they represent only a small fraction of the total student population, and thus sending children to schools cannot be the best method of educating them to be good members of society. I believe that parents play a more important role in teaching them how to be good citizens. In our country the average class size is 25-30 students, which makes it difficult for educators to provide proper schooling for each student. One-to-one lessons at home, on the other hand, allow children to progress faster. Furthermore, parents form stronger bonds with their offspring and thus, it is easier for them to shape children’s personalities at an early age. For example, by telling stories such as Robin Hood and Cinderella before bedtime, parents can instill a sense of compassion and integrity into them. These children are likely to become good members of society when they grow up.In conclusion, although sending children to school can be seen as a way of teaching them how to be good citizens, I believe that domestic upbringing has a bigger impact on determining who they are in the future.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hey! I completely understand you and feel sorry you have to experience the lackage of free time. It's an awfull state, when your life seems not to belong to yourself. I faced  with the same problem during my first year in the university and here are some tips that helped me to organise my free time and to lead a harmonical study-work-life balance:

First of all, timetables really work. And the last advice - always remember, that your assesments don't determine your personality. In a couple of years you will hardly remember a bad mark for a control work, about which you worry now. However, time flows very fast and your university life will come to the end quite soon. Try to live this chapter of your life in a such way, that in the future it will evoke only happy and exciting memories!
